content: Vancomycin AUC estimate now available. Level is trending high given worsening renal function.
⚠ Nephrotoxicity alert: Vancomycin + piperacillin-tazobactam in the setting of worsening AKI. Consider alternative gram-negative coverage.
CK level returned: 3× ULN. Daptomycin-associated myopathy is possible. Reassess continued use.
LFTs mildly elevated — AST/ALT 2× ULN. Consistent with known nafcillin hepatotoxicity pattern.
LFTs mildly elevated — AST/ALT 2× ULN. Consistent with known oxacillin hepatotoxicity pattern.
⚠ AKI has worsened further. SCr now 2.8. Vancomycin level critically elevated. Nephrotoxicity event in progress.
⚠ Blood cultures at day 5: Persistent Staphylococcus aureus bacteremia. Patient is febrile. Bacteremia has NOT cleared. Treatment failure event.

⚠ Relapse event: Patient returns 3 weeks after discharge with recurrent foot pain, fever, and rising inflammatory markers. MRI shows recurrent osteomyelitis. Treatment failure due to insufficient duration.
